1.title: A prophet we know little about
1.text: Joel is identified only as 'the son of Pethuel' (1:1); nothing else of his family, home town, or exact era is recorded. His message, not his biography, is what carries the book.

1.question: In Joel 1:4, four kinds of creatures devour the land in turn. What are they?

1.template: the drink offering is cut off from the house of the {0}

1.question: How does Joel 1:15 describe the day of the Lord?
options: As at hand, and as a destruction from the Almighty
As a day of harvest and abundance

1.question: In Joel 1:19-20, what has devoured the pastures of the wilderness?

2.question: In Joel 2:1, what are the people commanded to blow in Zion?
options: The trumpet, to sound an alarm

2.question: How does Joel 2:9 say the invaders will enter the houses?
options: In at the windows like a thief

2.template: And rend your {0}, and not your garments

2.question: In Joel 2:19, what three things does the Lord promise to send his people?
According to Joel 2:25, what does the Lord promise to restore?
options: The years that the locust hath eaten

2.question: Joel 2:28 promises that the Lord will pour out his spirit upon whom?

2.title: Quoted at Pentecost
2.text: Joel 2:28-32 — 'I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh' — was quoted by Peter in Acts 2 to explain the outpouring of the Holy Ghost on the day of Pentecost.

3.question: To what valley does the Lord bring the nations for judgment in Joel 3:2?

3.template: Beat your plowshares into {0}, and your pruninghooks into spears

3.question: In Joel 3:9-11, what are the nations summoned to prepare?
What does Joel 3:14 call the place where multitudes gather?

3.title: Water from the house of the Lord
3.text: Joel 3:18 pictures the mountains dropping new wine and a fountain flowing from the house of the Lord — an image of restored abundance echoed later by Ezekiel and in Revelation.

3.question: How does Joel 3:16 describe the Lord toward his own people, even as he judges the nations?
options: The hope of his people and the strength of Israel
